', '[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *liggitt* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=kubernetes%2Fkubernetes). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[pkg/apis/OWNERS](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/blob/master/pkg/apis/OWNERS)~~ [liggitt] - ~~[staging/src/k8s.io/api/OWNERS](https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/blob/master/staging/src/k8s.io/api/OWNERS)~~ [liggitt] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
', 'Let's look at what's happening and how we might do better: > inconsistent DV added to v1 without being added to other versions You added `maxItems=10`. As far as I can see that's not really a requirement -- the manual validation doesn't seem to enforce it and there's not a test for it in pkg/apis/admissionregistration/validation/validation_test.go -- in fact, it looks like there are NO success-tests which exercise the `Variables` field, and only a handful of error-tests. Let's assume that this was a real rule that manual validation actually did check. The first step of converting an existing field to DV *must* be "make sure the existing tests work". The very first DV PR was touching ReplicationController and those tests were TERRIBLE. The first commit in your PR should be fixing the existing tests to prove the boundaries of the existing validation. A better example would be to add `+k8s:maxItems=64` to `MatchConditions`. Now, I did that and (AFAICT) tests still pass which is suspect. There *is* a manual testcase for it, but there's not a DV test for it (this would be the first use of DV in that type). Let's try a different case. Add a `minItems` to `resource.DeviceClassSpec.Selectors[]`. Tests in `./pkg/registry/resource/deviceclass/` do fail. The standard test scaffold calls into `runtimetest.RunValidationForEachVersion()` which compares errors across API versions (which is kind of wrong on its own, since a field might be moved or renamed across versions, but it works for now). So the problem here is that you added it to one group and not others and there are no test cases to trigger it. Should we emit test-cases? Maybe but I don't see how we know what makes a valid object (short of interpreting all the tags in reverse and even that is never really going to work. I suppose we COULD have tests in every strategy which call `RunValidationForEachVersion()` with fuzzed input? I am not sure if that would explode somewhere else. But that would still be a randomized fuzz test, at best. So I am not sure I see what a reasonable way to catch this would be -- you added logic which has no tests and is never triggered. The others are similar in form -- adding code without tests can be "caught" by code-coverage tools, but that is not unique to DV. Perhaps we should invest in a single PR (or set of PRs) which wire up every type to DV, even if it has no tags, and puts the test scaffold in place. Then at least we don't have to worry about that being forgotten. The REAL problem here is that too much of the strategy is open-coded. ', "> Let's assume that this was a real rule that manual validation actually did check. this was less about testing preserving existing behavior in a manual->DV conversion, and more about experimenting with what I could rely on CI to catch when reviewing a DV tag in a new field", "> Perhaps we should invest in a single PR (or set of PRs) which wire up every type to DV, even if it has no tags, and puts the test scaffold in place. Then at least we don't have to worry about that being forgotten. Yeah, ensuring the pieces are at least connected for all types / resources / subresources we serve (either unconditionally or if the type has any DV anywhere, whichever makes more sense) seems like a good idea. ", 'That seems achievable. Then converting a field or adding a new one has one less step. ', "> inconsistent DV added to v1 without being added to other versions This would have been caught if a single invalid test case is present. For an invalid test case, the framework expects to achieve the same errors across all versions. So, if there were a single test case to test maxItems=10, the framework would show that v1beta1 didn't return the same error and fail. > DV added to go types without being wired up to actually be called by the strategy (and/or called by all subresource strategies, and/or called correctly without switching new/old object arg order, etc) Similar, a single test case would have achieved the same. DV tests invoke validations using strategy.go validations, not directly through the scheme or generated validations methods. So, this would have been caught.\xa0In the tests we would have to make sure all subresources are configured. We can see how we can make sure all subresources are configured in DV tests. > DV added without any corresponding test coverage This is important. We don't have a test for this. \xa0Right now, it's on the reviewers to make sure tests are present (and confugured properly). ", "> So the problem here is that you added it to one group and not others and there are no test cases to trigger it. Should we emit test-cases? Maybe but I don't see how we know what makes a valid object (short of interpreting all the tags in reverse and even that is never really going to work. I don't think we need to generate tests/testcases, but we _could_ collect the errors produced by the tests the developer provided and ensure there's a testcase triggering every field+rule's failure case. So in this PR's example, CI could ensure something like: 1. Tests exercising DV exist for these types 2. Somewhere in the table test, there's a testcase that produces a DV error on field `variables` with origin=maxItems That would catch absence of a DV test, and absence of a testcase that covers the failure scenario for the {field,DV-rule} tuple For bonus points, if DV testcase structure is required to provide at least one success case alongside failure cases, that would also poke the developer to think about testing the passing case > This would have been caught if a single invalid test case is present. For an invalid test case, the framework expects to achieve the same errors across all versions. So, if there were a single test case to test maxItems=10, the framework would show that v1beta1 didn't return the same error and fail. Depending on unit tests of the edges for every validation rule to notice DV drift between versions seems really fragile to me. For types where field structure/names are identical between versions, shouldn't we also be making sure DV matches between the versions to prevent accidents by default (with some way to ack intentional divergence)? I really thought that had been agreed to and already done as a prereq to making version-specific validation directives which make accidental divergence likely.", "> For types where field structure/names are identical between versions, shouldn't we also be making sure DV matches between the versions We do have that, but it's as a function of the outputs. There's not really any linkage between different apigroups at the code-generation level. It's not impossible, but it's not something we do anywhere else right now.", ">For types where field structure/names are identical between versions, shouldn't we also be making sure DV matches between the versions to prevent accidents by default (with some way to ack intentional divergence)? We actually have this. We have a fuzz test to ensure the same errors are returned for each APIVersion. \xa0It was not triggered for this case because of a configuration issue with\xa0fuzz testing, we are always\xa0setting number of items in the slice between 0 and 1. I have tried to fix this issue in\xa0https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/138032, This fix will prevent this. \xa0", "> > For types where field structure/names are identical between versions, shouldn't we also be making sure DV matches between the versions to prevent accidents by default (with some way to ack intentional divergence)? > > We actually have this. We have a fuzz test to ensure the same errors are returned for each APIVersion. Fuzz tests / hand-written unit tests of boundary conditions are good, but that's not the same as checking the DV directives actually match on corresponding fields between versions. I think we should do both.", 'I was going to say that we don't do that for +default but I realized that that is supposed to be different parade paper version. And then I realized that there are, at least hypothetically, cases where DV might be different. I know we have at least talked about how we could use API versions to do more severe forms of ratcheting. I can't find the details now, probably some slack thread, and I don't think we actually did it. Anyway, I see what you're asking for, but I'm not sure I buy it. It's not sufficient in all cases, but works in most, which sounds like setting some future developer up for a fall. More immediately, it sounds like a lot of work to that already has to be covered by human review, namely, "did you add tests?". Thinking through how to implement something like this, it seems fairly invasive. First we have to tag each API package in a family with some new tag, or extract it from the package name, but that seems fragile. Then, as we process each package in the tool, we have to save all of the state that we generate somewhere keyed by that family name. Then, after processing all packages, we have to walk each instance of a family and compare them to each other instance. We need another way to indicate that a field shouldn't be considered part of the family set, or something. The last part is moderately tricky, because we have to handle comparing type names across packages, make sure we handle loops in the graph, etc. nothing here is impossible, or even that hard really, but it's going to be tedious to get all the corner cases, and all the exception cases. We need to ensure that the "family"" tag is set on these packages, either by always requiring it with a bogus value when it's not actually useful, or by human review. I feel like a better return on investment would be to generate test cases which trigger the existing logic across versions. For example, if we see that a field is tagged with maxItems=10, we could automatically emit a test for 9, 10, 11. I am not sure if we even need to worry about the fact that zero value items are likely to be invalid in some other way, as long as all the versions return equivalent errors, including Max items. So in the example you have, it would have generated a test case that would have failed the equivalence. I think that could be entirely generated, and while field renaming will break the equivalence, we already have that problem, so when we finally hit it, we have to solve it once for both.', 'The single biggest risk I saw with DV was moving validation logic from "centralized code operating on the internal version which is always consistent by default across versions", to "annotations on per-version typed fields, which is inconsistent by default unless caught by tests". When fields are correlated across versions of a type, I am firmly of the opinion that validation should also be identical unless an exception is explicitly desired. I like fuzz / unit tests on the errors produced by validation, but I don't think they will catch all the drifts or make direct comparison of DV annotation consistency unnecessary. I can't find the threads / PRs where this was discussed, but I thought I repeatedly raised this as a risk and a requirement around DV, and I thought it got repeatedly acked.', 'https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/8550#issuecomment-1361496710 was one thread > @liggitt: > I think completely per-version validation will cause more bugs than it fixes. Keeping open-coded validation rules consistent between multiple versions sounds really difficult to get right (both to write and to test). > @thockin: > Then we have to keep the decalarations in sync across API versions, but hopefully we can automate most of that. other threads look to have been in person-to-person chats, and looking back, when I thought this was asked / answered, we were talking past each other (I meant actually checking the DV annotations matched, and the response was thinking about the unit/fuzz tests of behavior and errors produced). For example: ', "I *do* hear the risk, I'm not trying to be dismissive. I'm saying that I think the bigger RoI is (probably) in generating a test that we know will fail, and ensuring it fails the same way in all versions, rather than trying to correlate at the source level. Eventually we have to handle moved/renamed fields in this and it SEEMS (no data) easier to fuzzy-match an error across field names than to explicitly designate how a field maps between versions. But I have no concrete implementation for either, so ... We can't generate comprehensive tests, which require human review (for now?) but we can generate a tripwire test, I think.", 'I'd gladly take a simplistic "DV annotations match unless allowlisted to mismatch" guardrail even if it only works when types and field names match across versions (which is by far the majority case). I don't think that would stand in the way of generating test cases around boundary conditions, but I'm pretty skeptical that generated tests of boundary conditions will not hit the same cross-version correlation difficulties when types/fields don't match.']

', 'Let's look at what's happening and how we might do better: > inconsistent DV added to v1 without being added to other versions You added `maxItems=10`. As far as I can see that's not really a requirement -- the manual validation doesn't seem to enforce it and there's not a test for it in pkg/apis/admissionregistration/validation/validation_test.go -- in fact, it looks like there are NO success-tests which exercise the `Variables` field, and only a handful of error-tests. Let's assume that this was a real rule that manual validation actually did check. The first step of converting an existing field to DV *must* be "make sure the existing tests work". The very first DV PR was touching ReplicationController and those tests were TERRIBLE. The first commit in your PR should be fixing the existing tests to prove the boundaries of the existing validation. A better example would be to add `+k8s:maxItems=64` to `MatchConditions`. Now, I did that and (AFAICT) tests still pass which is suspect. There *is* a manual testcase for it, but there's not a DV test for it (this would be the first use of DV in that type). Let's try a different case. Add a `minItems` to `resource.DeviceClassSpec.Selectors[]`. Tests in `./pkg/registry/resource/deviceclass/` do fail. The standard test scaffold calls into `runtimetest.RunValidationForEachVersion()` which compares errors across API versions (which is kind of wrong on its own, since a field might be moved or renamed across versions, but it works for now). So the problem here is that you added it to one group and not others and there are no test cases to trigger it. Should we emit test-cases? Maybe but I don't see how we know what makes a valid object (short of interpreting all the tags in reverse and even that is never really going to work. I suppose we COULD have tests in every strategy which call `RunValidationForEachVersion()` with fuzzed input? I am not sure if that would explode somewhere else. But that would still be a randomized fuzz test, at best. So I am not sure I see what a reasonable way to catch this would be -- you added logic which has no tests and is never triggered. The others are similar in form -- adding code without tests can be "caught" by code-coverage tools, but that is not unique to DV. Perhaps we should invest in a single PR (or set of PRs) which wire up every type to DV, even if it has no tags, and puts the test scaffold in place. Then at least we don't have to worry about that being forgotten. The REAL problem here is that too much of the strategy is open-coded. ', "> Let's assume that this was a real rule that manual validation actually did check. this was less about testing preserving existing behavior in a manual->DV conversion, and more about experimenting with what I could rely on CI to catch when reviewing a DV tag in a new field", "> Perhaps we should invest in a single PR (or set of PRs) which wire up every type to DV, even if it has no tags, and puts the test scaffold in place. Then at least we don't have to worry about that being forgotten. Yeah, ensuring the pieces are at least connected for all types / resources / subresources we serve (either unconditionally or if the type has any DV anywhere, whichever makes more sense) seems like a good idea. ", 'That seems achievable. Then converting a field or adding a new one has one less step. ', "> inconsistent DV added to v1 without being added to other versions This would have been caught if a single invalid test case is present. For an invalid test case, the framework expects to achieve the same errors across all versions. So, if there were a single test case to test maxItems=10, the framework would show that v1beta1 didn't return the same error and fail. > DV added to go types without being wired up to actually be called by the strategy (and/or called by all subresource strategies, and/or called correctly without switching new/old object arg order, etc) Similar, a single test case would have achieved the same. ", "> So the problem here is that you added it to one group and not others and there are no test cases to trigger it. Should we emit test-cases? Maybe but I don't see how we know what makes a valid object (short of interpreting all the tags in reverse and even that is never really going to work. I don't think we need to generate tests/testcases, but we _could_ collect the errors produced by the tests the developer provided and ensure there's a testcase triggering every field+rule's failure case. So in this PR's example, CI could ensure something like: 1. Tests exercising DV exist for these types 2. Somewhere in the table test, there's a testcase that produces a DV error on field `variables` with origin=maxItems That would catch absence of a DV test, and absence of a testcase that covers the failure scenario for the {field,DV-rule} tuple For bonus points, if DV testcase structure is required to provide at least one success case alongside failure cases, that would also poke the developer to think about testing the passing case > This would have been caught if a single invalid test case is present. For an invalid test case, the framework expects to achieve the same errors across all versions. So, if there were a single test case to test maxItems=10, the framework would show that v1beta1 didn't return the same error and fail. Depending on unit tests of the edges for every validation rule to notice DV drift between versions seems really fragile to me. For types where field structure/names are identical between versions, shouldn't we also be making sure DV matches between the versions to prevent accidents by default (with some way to ack intentional divergence)? I really thought that had been agreed to and already done as a prereq to making version-specific validation directives which make accidental divergence likely.", "> For types where field structure/names are identical between versions, shouldn't we also be making sure DV matches between the versions We do have that, but it's as a function of the outputs. There's not really any linkage between different apigroups at the code-generation level. It's not impossible, but it's not something we do anywhere else right now.", ">For types where field structure/names are identical between versions, shouldn't we also be making sure DV matches between the versions to prevent accidents by default (with some way to ack intentional divergence)? We actually have this. We have a fuzz test to ensure the same errors are returned for each APIVersion. \xa0It was not triggered for this case because of a configuration issue with\xa0fuzz testing, we are always\xa0setting number of items in the slice between 0 and 1. I have tried to fix this issue in\xa0https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/pull/138032, This fix will prevent this. \xa0", "> > For types where field structure/names are identical between versions, shouldn't we also be making sure DV matches between the versions to prevent accidents by default (with some way to ack intentional divergence)? > > We actually have this. We have a fuzz test to ensure the same errors are returned for each APIVersion. Fuzz tests / hand-written unit tests of boundary conditions are good, but that's not the same as checking the DV directives actually match on corresponding fields between versions. I think we should do both.", 'I was going to say that we don't do that for +default but I realized that that is supposed to be different parade paper version. And then I realized that there are, at least hypothetically, cases where DV might be different. I know we have at least talked about how we could use API versions to do more severe forms of ratcheting. I can't find the details now, probably some slack thread, and I don't think we actually did it. Anyway, I see what you're asking for, but I'm not sure I buy it. It's not sufficient in all cases, but works in most, which sounds like setting some future developer up for a fall. More immediately, it sounds like a lot of work to that already has to be covered by human review, namely, "did you add tests?". Thinking through how to implement something like this, it seems fairly invasive. First we have to tag each API package in a family with some new tag, or extract it from the package name, but that seems fragile. Then, as we process each package in the tool, we have to save all of the state that we generate somewhere keyed by that family name. Then, after processing all packages, we have to walk each instance of a family and compare them to each other instance. We need another way to indicate that a field shouldn't be considered part of the family set, or something. The last part is moderately tricky, because we have to handle comparing type names across packages, make sure we handle loops in the graph, etc. nothing here is impossible, or even that hard really, but it's going to be tedious to get all the corner cases, and all the exception cases. We need to ensure that the "family"" tag is set on these packages, either by always requiring it with a bogus value when it's not actually useful, or by human review. I feel like a better return on investment would be to generate test cases which trigger the existing logic across versions. For example, if we see that a field is tagged with maxItems=10, we could automatically emit a test for 9, 10, 11. I am not sure if we even need to worry about the fact that zero value items are likely to be invalid in some other way, as long as all the versions return equivalent errors, including Max items. So in the example you have, it would have generated a test case that would have failed the equivalence. I think that could be entirely generated, and while field renaming will break the equivalence, we already have that problem, so when we finally hit it, we have to solve it once for both.', 'The single biggest risk I saw with DV was moving validation logic from "centralized code operating on the internal version which is always consistent by default across versions", to "annotations on per-version typed fields, which is inconsistent by default unless caught by tests". When fields are correlated across versions of a type, I am firmly of the opinion that validation should also be identical unless an exception is explicitly desired. I like fuzz / unit tests on the errors produced by validation, but I don't think they will catch all the drifts or make direct comparison of DV annotation consistency unnecessary. I can't find the threads / PRs where this was discussed, but I thought I repeatedly raised this as a risk and a requirement around DV, and I thought it got repeatedly acked.', 'https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/8550#issuecomment-1361496710 was one thread > @liggitt: > I think completely per-version validation will cause more bugs than it fixes. Keeping open-coded validation rules consistent between multiple versions sounds really difficult to get right (both to write and to test). > @thockin: > Then we have to keep the decalarations in sync across API versions, but hopefully we can automate most of that. other threads look to have been in person-to-person chats, and looking back, when I thought this was asked / answered, we were talking past each other (I meant actually checking the DV annotations matched, and the response was thinking about the unit/fuzz tests of behavior and errors produced). For example: ', "I *do* hear the risk, I'm not trying to be dismissive. I'm saying that I think the bigger RoI is (probably) in generating a test that we know will fail, and ensuring it fails the same way in all versions, rather than trying to correlate at the source level. Eventually we have to handle moved/renamed fields in this and it SEEMS (no data) easier to fuzzy-match an error across field names than to explicitly designate how a field maps between versions. But I have no concrete implementation for either, so ... We can't generate comprehensive tests, which require human review (for now?) but we can generate a tripwire test, I think.", 'I'd gladly take a simplistic "DV annotations match unless allowlisted to mismatch" guardrail even if it only works when types and field names match across versions (which is by far the majority case). I don't think that would stand in the way of generating test cases around boundary conditions, but I'm pretty skeptical that generated tests of boundary conditions will not hit the same cross-version correlation difficulties when types/fields don't match.']

commits:
[{'sha': 'a2258d1cf6a8d118a677e00eb4bd8c92a5ac1f4d', 'message': 'pkg/registry/core/pod/storage: add PDB cause type to eviction forbidden errors

When checkAndDecrement returns a Forbidden error due to negative
DisruptionsAllowed or an oversized DisruptedPods map, clients had no
way to distinguish these from other Forbidden errors without
string-matching on the message.

Populate StatusCause with the existing DisruptionBudgetCause on both
Forbidden error paths, consistent with the existing pattern on the
TooManyRequests path.

Fixes: https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/125500'}]

review:
[
  {
    "type": "Bug",
    "description": "在 `checkAndDecrement` 函数中，当 `pdb.Status.DisruptionsAllowed < 0` 时，构造的错误消息末尾有一个多余的句点（'.'）。虽然这不会导致功能错误，但破坏了项目内错误消息格式的一致性。Kubernetes 项目对错误消息的格式有严格要求，以确保日志和用户界面的清晰度。",
    "evidence": "文件 `pkg/registry/core/pod/storage/eviction.go` 第 430 行：`fmt.Errorf(\"pdb disruptions allowed is negative.\")`。对比同一函数中其他错误消息（如第 438 行）和项目中的其他错误消息，通常不在 `fmt.Errorf` 的消息末尾添加句点。",
    "advice": "移除错误消息末尾的句点，将代码改为 `fmt.Errorf(\"pdb disruptions allowed is negative\")`，以保持与项目其他部分错误消息格式的一致性。",
    "confidence": 0.9,
    "severity": "Low"
  },
  {
    "type": "Readability",
    "description": "在测试文件 `eviction_test.go` 中新增的测试用例 `\"matching pdbs with negative disruptions allowed, pod running\"` 和 `\"matching pdbs with too many disrupted pods, pod running\"` 里，`expectError` 字段的字符串非常长且包含了完整的错误消息。这降低了测试的可读性，并且当错误消息格式发生微小变化时，测试容易失败。Kubernetes 项目通常倾向于在测试中验证错误的关键属性（如类型、原因），而不是完整的字符串匹配。",
    "evidence": "文件 `pkg/registry/core/pod/storage/eviction_test.go` 第 558 行：`expectError: `poddisruptionbudget.policy \"foo\" is forbidden: pdb disruptions allowed is negative: Forbidden: The disruption budget foo does not allow evicting pods currently: pdb disruptions allowed is negative`,` 和第 571 行：`expectError: `poddisruptionbudget.policy \"foo\" is forbidden: DisruptedPods map too big - too many evictions not confirmed by PDB controller: Forbidden: The disruption budget foo does not allow evicting pods currently: too many pending evictions not confirmed by PDB controller`,`。这些字符串包含了错误状态、原因和消息的拼接，过于冗长。",
    "advice": "考虑简化测试断言。既然新增的测试用例已经通过 `expectedCause` 字段和新增的检查逻辑（第 673-677 行）来验证错误原因（`HasStatusCause`），可以移除或缩短 `expectError` 字符串，只检查错误类型（如 `Forbidden`）或使用更灵活的正则匹配。这能提高测试的健壮性和可读性。",
    "confidence": 0.7,
    "severity": "Low"
  }
]
commits:
[{'sha': 'a2258d1cf6a8d118a677e00eb4bd8c92a5ac1f4d', 'message': 'pkg/registry/core/pod/storage: add PDB cause type to eviction forbidden errors

When checkAndDecrement returns a Forbidden error due to negative
DisruptionsAllowed or an oversized DisruptedPods map, clients had no
way to distinguish these from other Forbidden errors without
string-matching on the message.

Populate StatusCause with the existing DisruptionBudgetCause on both
Forbidden error paths, consistent with the existing pattern on the
TooManyRequests path.

Fixes: https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/issues/125500'}]

review:
[
  {
    "type": "Bug",
    "description": "在 `hns.getAllEndpointsByNetwork` 方法中，当发现重复IP的远程端点时，会将其ID添加到 `remoteEPsWithDupIP` 映射中，以便后续删除。然而，在 `proxier.syncProxyRules` 中，对 `hns.deleteAllRemoteEndpointsWithDupIP` 的调用使用了 `defer`。在Kubernetes的代理组件中，`syncProxyRules` 会被频繁调用。使用 `defer` 意味着删除操作会延迟到函数返回时才执行。如果函数在后续逻辑中出错提前返回，或者函数被频繁调用，可能会导致重复IP的远程端点未能被及时清理，或者删除操作被不必要地延迟，影响系统的稳定性和正确性。这与项目旨在可靠管理Windows HNS端点、解决Windows bug导致重复IP端点的设计目标相悖。",
    "evidence": "文件: pkg/proxy/winkernel/proxier.go, 行: 1174\n+	defer hns.deleteAllRemoteEndpointsWithDupIP(remoteEPsWithDupIP)",
    "advice": "建议立即执行删除操作，而不是使用 `defer`。可以将 `defer` 行替换为直接调用：`hns.deleteAllRemoteEndpointsWithDupIP(remoteEPsWithDupIP)`。这确保了重复的远程端点在查询后立即被清理，符合及时修复Windows bug导致的残留端点的意图。",
    "confidence": 0.9,
    "severity": "Medium"
  },
  {
    "type": "Readability",
    "description": "在 `hns.getAllEndpointsByNetwork` 方法的注释中，返回值的描述提到返回一个由端点ID和IP地址共同作为键的映射。然而，实际的代码实现中，映射 `endpointInfos` 的键既可以是端点ID（`ep.Id`），也可以是IP地址（`ipConfig.IpAddress`）。注释的表述（\"keyed by both endpoint ID and IP address\"）可能引起误解，让人以为键是一个复合键，而实际上是通过两个不同的键（ID或IP）将同一个端点对象放入映射两次。这降低了代码注释的清晰度。",
    "evidence": "文件: pkg/proxy/winkernel/hns.go, 行: 32-33\n+	// Returns a map of endpoints keyed by both endpoint ID and IP address for all endpoints on the specified network, and a map of remote endpoints with duplicate IPs to be deleted.\n+	getAllEndpointsByNetwork(networkName string) (map[string]*endpointInfo, map[string]bool, error)",
    "advice": "建议更新方法注释，使其更准确地反映实现：返回的映射中，每个端点会以两种键（其HNS ID和其IP地址）存储。例如：\"Returns a map where each endpoint is stored under two keys: its HNS ID and its IP address. Also returns a map of remote endpoint IDs with duplicate IPs to be deleted.\"",
    "confidence": 0.8,
    "severity": "Low"
  },
  {
    "type": "Bug",
    "description": "在 `hns.getAllEndpointsByNetwork` 方法的重复IP处理逻辑中，当遇到一个本地端点（`curEpIsLocal`为true）且映射中已存在一个具有相同IP的远程端点（`existingEp.isLocal`为false）时，代码会标记远程端点待删除（`remoteEPsWithDupIP[existingEp.hnsID] = true`），并从映射中删除该远程端点的两个条目（通过ID和IP）。然而，它随后通过 `delete(endpointInfos, existingEp.ip)` 删除IP键。此处的 `existingEp.ip` 与当前正在处理的 `ipConfig.IpAddress` 是相同的IP。紧接着，代码会“fall through to add local”，为这个相同的IP创建一个新的本地端点条目并添加到映射中。虽然逻辑意图正确，但先删除IP键再立即重新添加可能略显冗余，且依赖于 `delete` 操作在循环中立即生效。在复杂的并发或未来修改场景下，这种模式可能引入细微错误。虽然当前未发现功能问题，但代码结构可以更清晰。",
    "evidence": "文件: pkg/proxy/winkernel/hns.go, 行: 151-159\n+				if curEpIsLocal && !existingEp.isLocal {\n+\t\t\t\t\t// Local found, stale remote in map → delete remote from HNS, overwrite\n+\t\t\t\t\tremoteEPsWithDupIP[existingEp.hnsID] = true\n+\t\t\t\t\tdelete(endpointInfos, existingEp.hnsID)\n+\t\t\t\t\tdelete(endpointInfos, existingEp.ip)\n+\t\t\t\t\t// fall through to add local\n+\t\t\t\t}",
    "advice": "建议重构此部分逻辑以提高清晰度。可以明确地在处理完远程端点后，直接创建并添加本地端点信息到映射中，避免先删除IP键再添加。或者，可以添加注释明确说明此IP键将被新的本地端点条目立即覆盖。保持代码意图清晰，便于后续维护。",
    "confidence": 0.7,
    "severity": "Low"
  },
  {
    "type": "Design",
    "description": "新增的 `deleteAllRemoteEndpointsWithDupIP` 方法及其在 `getAllEndpointsByNetwork` 中的使用，引入了一种新的错误处理模式：在后台静默记录错误（仅通过klog.ErrorS），而不是将错误传播给调用者。这与项目中许多其他HNS操作方法（如 `deleteEndpoint`）通常返回错误给调用者的模式不一致。这种不一致性可能会使调用者难以感知和处理底层HNS操作失败的情况，可能掩盖一些需要上层逻辑处理的持久性问题。",
    "evidence": "文件: pkg/proxy/winkernel/hns.go, 行: 189-197\n+func (hns hns) deleteAllRemoteEndpointsWithDupIP(remoteEPsWithDupIP map[string]bool) {\n+\tfor hnsID := range remoteEPsWithDupIP {\n+\t\tklog.V(3).InfoS(\"Deleting stale remote endpoint with duplicate IP\", \"hnsID\", hnsID)\n+\t\terr := hns.deleteEndpoint(hnsID)\n+\t\tif err != nil {\n+\t\t\tklog.ErrorS(err, \"Failed to delete stale remote endpoint with duplicate IP\", \"hnsID\", hnsID)\n+\t\t}\n+\t}\n+}",
    "advice": "考虑是否应该修改 `deleteAllRemoteEndpointsWithDupIP` 的签名以返回一个聚合错误，或者至少记录一个警告级别的日志，以便运维人员能够注意到持续的删除失败。需要评估在 `syncProxyRules` 的上下文中，此类失败的最佳处理方式是什么，并与项目其他部分的错误处理模式保持一致。",
    "confidence": 0.75,
    "severity": "Medium"
  }
]

[{'sha': '859947e4d5d25bde62ed65288adea15b07705bd1', 'message': 'kubelet: fix nil pointer panic in doPodResizeAction when cgroupsPerQOS=false

When cgroupsPerQOS is disabled, the kubelet uses podContainerManagerNoop whose
GetPodCgroupConfig returns (nil, nil). doPodResizeAction then dereferences the
nil ResourceConfig fields (Memory, CPUQuota, CPUShares) causing a panic during
in-place pod vertical scaling.

Track whether pod cgroups are actually managed and skip pod-level cgroup updates
in resizeContainers when they are not, while still allowing container-level
resizes to proceed. Default nil configs to empty ResourceConfig for safe field
access in downstream comparisons.

Signed-off-by: Rajneesh Chaudhary <rajneeshrehsaan48@gmail.com>'}]

    "type": "Bug",
    "description": "在 `resizeContainers` 函数中，当 `podCgroupsManaged` 为 `false` 时，会跳过所有 pod-level cgroup 的更新。然而，在 CPU 资源调整的代码块中，传递给 `resizeContainers` 的 `desiredCPUShares` 参数直接解引用了 `podResources.CPUShares` 指针，而没有检查其是否为 nil。当 `podResources.CPUShares` 为 nil 时，解引用会导致 panic。这与修复 nil 指针 panic 的提交目标相冲突。",
    "evidence": "文件: pkg/kubelet/kuberuntime/kuberuntime_manager.go, 行: 1023\n`int64(*podResources.CPUShares)`",
    "advice": "在解引用 `podResources.CPUShares` 之前，应添加 nil 检查，类似于对 `podResources.Memory` 和 `podResources.CPUQuota` 的处理。例如：`var desiredCPUShares int64; if podResources.CPUShares != nil { desiredCPUShares = int64(*podResources.CPUShares) }`。",
    "confidence": 0.95,
    "severity": "High"
  },
  {
    "type": "Readability",
    "description": "变量命名 `podCgroupsManaged` 的语义不够清晰。它实际上表示“pod-level cgroups 是否被管理”，但这个名字可能被误解为“pod cgroups 是否正在被管理（即当前操作）”。在 Kubernetes 代码库中，对于布尔标志，更清晰的命名可能是 `podCgroupsEnabled` 或 `podCgroupManagementEnabled`，以明确表示一种状态而非动作。",
    "evidence": "文件: pkg/kubelet/kuberuntime/kuberuntime_manager.go, 行: 851\n`podCgroupsManaged := currentPodMemoryConfig != nil && currentPodCPUConfig != nil`",
    "advice": "考虑将变量名 `podCgroupsManaged` 重命名为更具描述性的名称，例如 `podCgroupManagementEnabled` 或 `podLevelCgroupsExist`，以更准确地反映其含义（即 pod 级别的 cgroup 配置是否存在/被管理）。",
    "confidence": 0.7,
    "severity": "Low"
  },
  {
    "type": "Design",
    "description": "在 `resizeContainers` 函数内部，对 `podCgroupsManaged` 的检查被重复了四次（分别在 upsizing limits, upsizing requests, downsizing requests, downsizing limits 的条件判断中）。这种重复增加了代码的复杂性和维护成本。可以考虑将 `podCgroupsManaged` 的检查上移一层，或者重构 `setPodCgroupConfig` 的调用逻辑，以避免重复的条件判断。",
    "evidence": "文件: pkg/kubelet/kuberuntime/kuberuntime_manager.go, 行: 970, 977, 990, 997\n`if podCgroupsManaged && newPodCgLimValue > currPodCgLimValue { ... }` (共四次类似检查)",
    "advice": "可以考虑重构 `resizeContainers` 函数。例如，在函数开始处检查 `if !podCgroupsManaged { return nil }`，如果 pod cgroups 未被管理，则直接返回，避免后续所有 pod-level 的 cgroup 更新逻辑。或者，将 pod-level 的更新逻辑提取到一个辅助函数中，该函数仅在 `podCgroupsManaged` 为 true 时被调用。",
    "confidence": 0.8,
    "severity": "Low"
  }
]

  {
    "type": "Bug",
    "description": "测试逻辑存在缺陷。在收到第一个事件后，代码立即调用 `watcher.Stop()` 并休眠1秒。`watcher.Stop()` 会关闭结果通道，导致 `for` 循环在通道关闭后立即退出，无法继续处理通道中可能已缓冲的后续事件。因此，`watched` 计数很可能永远达不到 `expectWatched`（11），导致测试失败。",
    "evidence": "在 `if watched == 1 {` 代码块内，调用了 `watcher.Stop()`。这发生在从 `watcher.ResultChan()` 读取事件的循环内部。",
    "advice": "将 `watcher.Stop()` 的调用移出事件处理循环。一个可能的方案是：在启动一个 goroutine 来读取事件的同时，在主 goroutine 中等待一段时间（例如1秒），然后停止观察者。或者，在停止观察者之前，确保已经读取了足够数量的事件。",
    "confidence": 0.95,
    "severity": "High"
  },
  {
    "type": "Readability",
    "description": "变量命名 `expectWatched` 的语义不够清晰。`outBufferSize + 1` 中的 `+ 1` 代表“初始事件”，但这个含义没有在变量名或注释中明确体现，使得期望值的计算逻辑不易理解。",
    "evidence": "`expectWatched := outBufferSize + 1 // initial event`",
    "advice": "考虑将变量名改为 `minExpectedEvents` 或类似名称，并添加更清晰的注释来解释为什么期望值是缓冲区大小加一（例如，因为第一个事件在停止前被处理，并且缓冲区可能已满）。",
    "confidence": 0.8,
    "severity": "Low"
  },
  {
    "type": "Performance",
    "description": "在测试中使用了固定的 `time.Sleep(time.Second)`。这是一种不可靠的同步机制，可能导致测试在负载较重的环境下变慢或不稳定（flaky）。",
    "evidence": "`time.Sleep(time.Second)`",
    "advice": "避免在测试中使用固定的休眠。可以考虑使用更确定性的同步方式，例如使用通道、条件变量，或者至少使用一个可配置的超时时间，并通过轮询或 `context.WithTimeout` 来等待特定条件满足。",
    "confidence": 0.85,
    "severity": "Medium"
  }
]

    "evidence": "`watched == 1` 条件内包含 `time.Sleep` 和 `watcher.Stop()`，而循环继续读取事件。注释提到“在停止观察者之前等待事件到达并给它们一些时间填充队列”，但实现方式可能令人困惑。",
    "advice": "建议重构代码以分离关注点：明确等待初始事件到达的逻辑，然后停止观察者，再检查剩余事件。使用辅助函数或更清晰的循环结构可以提高可读性。",
    "confidence": 0.7,

  },

    "advice": "建议将 `outBufferSize` 定义为常量或从观察者配置中获取，以提高可维护性。同时，确保测试注释清楚地解释为什么期望这个特定数量的事件。",

  {
    "type": "Bug",
    "description": "代码变更的逻辑存在错误。当 `kubeletArguments.enablePodLevelResources` 为 `false` 时，代码意图是将 `InPlacePodLevelResourcesVerticalScaling` 也设置为 `false`。然而，代码中直接使用了 `kubeletArguments.enablePodLevelResources` 的值（即 `false`）来设置 `InPlacePodLevelResourcesVerticalScaling`。虽然当前值也是 `false`，但这依赖于 `kubeletArguments` 结构体中 `enablePodLevelResources` 字段的语义。如果未来 `kubeletArguments` 结构体发生变化，或者 `enablePodLevelResources` 的默认值不是 `false`，或者存在一个独立的 `enableInPlacePodLevelResourcesVerticalScaling` 字段，此逻辑将不正确。更健壮和清晰的写法是显式地设置为 `false`。",
    "evidence": "在 `test/e2e_node/cpu_manager_test.go` 和 `test/e2e_node/memory_manager_test.go` 中，新增的代码为：`newCfg.FeatureGates[\"InPlacePodLevelResourcesVerticalScaling\"] = kubeletArguments.enablePodLevelResources`。当 `enablePodLevelResources` 为 `false` 时，这确实会将 `InPlacePodLevelResourcesVerticalScaling` 设置为 `false`，但逻辑上不够清晰和直接。",
    "advice": "建议将赋值语句改为显式地设置为 `false`，例如：`newCfg.FeatureGates[\"InPlacePodLevelResourcesVerticalScaling\"] = false`。这样代码意图更明确，不依赖于 `enablePodLevelResources` 字段的当前值，也避免了未来字段语义变更带来的潜在风险。",
    "confidence": 0.8,
    "severity": "Low"
  },
  {
    "type": "Readability",
    "description": "代码变更在两个文件中重复了完全相同的逻辑。这违反了 DRY（Don't Repeat Yourself）原则，增加了维护成本。如果未来需要修改此逻辑（例如，添加更多依赖条件），必须在两个地方进行相同的修改，容易遗漏导致不一致。",
    "evidence": "相同的代码块出现在 `test/e2e_node/cpu_manager_test.go` 和 `test/e2e_node/memory_manager_test.go` 两个文件中：`if !kubeletArguments.enablePodLevelResources { newCfg.FeatureGates[\"InPlacePodLevelResourcesVerticalScaling\"] = kubeletArguments.enablePodLevelResources }`。",
    "advice": "考虑将此逻辑抽取到一个公共的辅助函数中，供两个测试文件调用。或者，如果这两个配置函数（`configureCPUManagerInKubelet` 和 `configureMemoryManagerInKubelet`）有共同的父级调用者或共享的配置结构，可以在更高层次统一处理此逻辑。这符合 Kubernetes 项目对代码可维护性的要求。",
    "confidence": 0.9,
    "severity": "Medium"
  },
  {
    "type": "Design",
    "description": "代码变更的逻辑不完整。注释说明 `InPlacePodLevelResourcesVerticalScaling` 仅在 `PodLevelResources` 启用时才受支持。当前的实现只在 `PodLevelResources` 为 `false` 时将其强制设为 `false`。然而，当 `PodLevelResources` 为 `true` 时，`InPlacePodLevelResourcesVerticalScaling` 的值应该由 `kubeletArguments` 中对应的字段（例如 `enableInPlacePodLevelResourcesVerticalScaling`）决定，但代码变更中并未处理这种情况。这可能导致当 `PodLevelResources` 启用时，`InPlacePodLevelResourcesVerticalScaling` 的 FeatureGate 未被正确设置（可能保持默认值或之前的值），从而影响测试的预期行为。",
    "evidence": "新增代码仅包含一个 `if` 语句：`if !kubeletArguments.enablePodLevelResources { ... }`。当 `enablePodLevelResources` 为 `true` 时，没有对 `InPlacePodLevelResourcesVerticalScaling` 进行任何赋值。结合项目背景，Kubernetes 的 e2e 测试通常需要精确控制 FeatureGate 的状态以确保测试覆盖特定场景。",
    "advice": "需要补充当 `enablePodLevelResources` 为 `true` 时的逻辑。应该检查 `kubeletArguments` 中是否存在一个控制 `InPlacePodLevelResourcesVerticalScaling` 的独立字段（例如 `enableInPlacePodLevelResourcesVerticalScaling`），如果存在，则使用该字段的值进行设置。如果不存在这样的字段，则需要根据测试设计决定其默认值（例如，也设置为 `true` 或从其他配置读取）。请查阅项目代码库，确认 `kubeletArguments` 结构体的完整定义。",
    "confidence": 0.7,
    "severity": "Medium"
  }
]

  {
    "type": "Bug",
    "description": "在 `ToSelectableFields` 函数中，`objectMetaFieldsSet` 的初始容量被设置为 2，但随后只添加了 `spec.nodeName` 一个字段。`generic.AddObjectMetaFieldsSet` 函数会添加多个元数据字段（如 `metadata.name`），这可能导致切片在追加时重新分配内存，使得初始容量设置变得多余且可能产生误导。虽然这不是一个功能错误，但代码意图不清晰，可能影响可读性。",
    "evidence": "文件: pkg/registry/storage/volumeattachment/strategy.go\n+func ToSelectableFields(va *storage.VolumeAttachment) fields.Set {\n+\t// Allocate with enough capacity for the fields we add plus\n+\t// the object meta fields added by AddObjectMetaFieldsSet.\n+\tobjectMetaFieldsSet := make(fields.Set, 2)\n+\tobjectMetaFieldsSet[\"spec.nodeName\"] = va.Spec.NodeName\n+\t// false = non-namespaced resource (VolumeAttachments are cluster-scoped)\n+\treturn generic.AddObjectMetaFieldsSet(objectMetaFieldsSet, &va.ObjectMeta, false)\n+}",
    "advice": "建议将初始容量设置为一个更合理的值（例如 5 或 10），以容纳 `AddObjectMetaFieldsSet` 添加的多个字段，或者直接使用 `make(fields.Set)` 让 Go 语言自动管理容量，使代码更简洁。",
    "confidence": 0.8,
    "severity": "Low"
  },
  {
    "type": "Bug",
    "description": "`NodeNameTriggerFunc` 函数在将 `runtime.Object` 类型断言为 `*storage.VolumeAttachment` 时，如果传入的对象不是 `*storage.VolumeAttachment` 类型，将会导致 panic。这与 `GetAttrs` 和 `NodeNameIndexFunc` 函数的行为不一致，后两者在类型断言失败时会返回错误。",
    "evidence": "文件: pkg/registry/storage/volumeattachment/strategy.go\n+// NodeNameTriggerFunc returns the spec.nodeName of the given object.\n+// It is used as a trigger function for the watch cache index.\n+func NodeNameTriggerFunc(obj runtime.Object) string {\n+\treturn obj.(*storage.VolumeAttachment).Spec.NodeName\n+}",
    "advice": "建议在函数中添加类型检查，并在类型不匹配时返回空字符串或 panic 并附带更清晰的错误信息，以保持与代码库中其他类似函数（如 `NodeNameIndexFunc`）的一致性。例如：`va, ok := obj.(*storage.VolumeAttachment); if !ok { return \"\" }`。",
    "confidence": 0.9,
    "severity": "Medium"
  },
  {
    "type": "Readability",
    "description": "在 `ToSelectableFields` 函数中，注释 `// false = non-namespaced resource (VolumeAttachments are cluster-scoped)` 是对 `AddObjectMetaFieldsSet` 函数 `false` 参数的说明，但该注释紧跟在 `objectMetaFieldsSet[\"spec.nodeName\"] = va.Spec.NodeName` 赋值语句之后，位置容易引起误解。注释应该紧邻其解释的代码。",
    "evidence": "文件: pkg/registry/storage/volumeattachment/strategy.go\n+\tobjectMetaFieldsSet[\"spec.nodeName\"] = va.Spec.NodeName\n+\t// false = non-namespaced resource (VolumeAttachments are cluster-scoped)\n+\treturn generic.AddObjectMetaFieldsSet(objectMetaFieldsSet, &va.ObjectMeta, false)",
    "advice": "建议将注释移动到 `return` 语句的同一行或上一行，紧靠 `false` 参数，以提高代码可读性。例如：`return generic.AddObjectMetaFieldsSet(objectMetaFieldsSet, &va.ObjectMeta, false) // false because VolumeAttachments are cluster-scoped`。",
    "confidence": 0.9,
    "severity": "Low"
  }
]

[{'sha': '8a8e9ef46ecc16faa46d3f69c38436cb8b63790b', 'message': 'Add field selector support for VolumeAttachment by spec.nodeName

VolumeAttachment currently only supports full list without any field
selector filtering. This is inefficient for components that only need
to list VolumeAttachments for a specific node, as they must fetch all
VolumeAttachments cluster-wide and filter client-side.

  }

[
  {
    "type": "Bug",
    "description": "逻辑条件判断错误。变更将清理陈旧连接条目的条件从 `endpoint.IsServing()` 改为 `endpoint.IsServing() || !endpoint.IsTerminating()`。这意味着一个端点只要处于 `IsTerminating() == false` 状态（即非终止状态），即使它 `IsServing() == false`（即不提供服务），也会被包含在有效端点集合中。这可能导致清理逻辑错误地保留那些指向不提供服务且非终止状态端点的连接条目，而这些条目本应被清理。这与提交信息中‘为 non-terminating endpoints 保留 UDP 连接条目’的意图不符，因为‘non-terminating’应该隐含‘serving’。当前逻辑会错误地包含‘non-serving and non-terminating’的端点。",
    "evidence": "文件: pkg/proxy/conntrack/cleanup.go
@@ -72,15 +72,7 @@ func CleanStaleEntries(ct Interface, ipFamily v1.IPFamily,
 
 		endpoints := sets.New[string]()
 		for _, endpoint := range endpointsMap[svcName] {
-			// We need to remove all the conntrack entries for a Service (IP or NodePort)
-			// that are not pointing to a serving endpoint.
-			// We map all the serving endpoints of the service and clear all the conntrack
-			// entries which are destined for the service and are not DNATed to these endpoints.
-			// Changes to the service should not affect existing flows, so we do not take
-			// traffic policies, topology, or terminating status of the service into account.
-			// This ensures that the behavior of UDP services remains consistent with TCP
-			// services.
-			if endpoint.IsServing() {
+			if endpoint.IsServing() || !endpoint.IsTerminating() {
 				portStr := strconv.Itoa(int(endpoint.Port()))
 				endpoints.Insert(net.JoinHostPort(endpoint.IP(), portStr))

    "description": "代码变更将清理陈旧连接跟踪条目的条件从 `endpoint.IsServing()` 修改为 `endpoint.IsServing() || !endpoint.IsTerminating()`。这可能导致一个逻辑错误：对于一个 `IsTerminating()` 返回 `true` 的端点，`!endpoint.IsTerminating()` 为 `false`，因此整个条件退化为 `endpoint.IsServing()`。然而，提交信息的意图是保留非终止（non-terminating）端点的 UDP 连接跟踪条目，即使它们当前不处于服务状态（例如，负载卸载场景）。当前的逻辑可能无法正确识别并保留那些 `IsServing() == false` 但 `IsTerminating() == false` 的端点。这可能导致为这些端点建立的现有 UDP 连接被错误地清理，破坏连接并影响服务可用性。",
    "evidence": "在 `pkg/proxy/conntrack/cleanup.go` 第 75 行，条件从 `if endpoint.IsServing() {` 更改为 `if endpoint.IsServing() || !endpoint.IsTerminating() {`。测试文件 `pkg/proxy/conntrack/cleanup_test.go` 第 155 行和第 243-246 行添加了对非服务但非终止端点的测试，以及第 273 行和第 284 行的注释表明意图是保留非终止端点的流。",
    "advice": "建议重新评估逻辑。意图似乎是保留那些 `IsTerminating() == false` 的端点，无论其服务状态如何。因此，条件应更改为 `if !endpoint.IsTerminating() {`，或者为了清晰起见，可以写成 `if endpoint.IsServing() || (endpoint.IsServing() == false && endpoint.IsTerminating() == false) {`。然而，根据 Kubernetes 端点的生命周期，一个端点可能处于 `Serving: false, Terminating: false` 状态（例如，准备服务但尚未就绪）。变更的目标是保留这些端点的连接。因此，最准确的逻辑可能是：如果端点不是终止状态（`!endpoint.IsTerminating()`），则将其视为有效端点以保留连接跟踪条目。请结合项目关于端点终止和连接保持的上下文进行确认。",

    "evidence": "在 `pkg/proxy/conntrack/cleanup.go` 第 62-63 行，注释从“to the set of serving endpoints”更改为“to the set of endpoints”。第 65 行类似。第 124 行和第 133 行的注释也从“represent a serving endpoint”更改为“represent an endpoint”。",
    "advice": "建议更新注释以反映新的逻辑。例如，在第 62-63 行附近添加注释，说明映射现在包含服务端点以及非终止的非服务端点，以保留 UDP 连接。例如：`// to the set of endpoints that should preserve conntrack entries (serving or non-terminating).`",
    "confidence": 0.9,

  {
    "type": "Design",
    "description": "变更修改了连接跟踪清理的核心逻辑，可能影响 UDP 服务的连接保持行为。虽然提交信息表明意图是保留非终止端点的 UDP 连接，但变更可能引入不一致性：TCP 服务的行为是否也应考虑终止状态？项目背景中强调“确保 UDP 服务的行为与 TCP 服务保持一致”，但此变更可能使 UDP 与 TCP 在处理非终止、非服务端点时产生差异，除非 TCP 的类似逻辑也同步更新。需要评估这是否符合项目的整体设计目标。",

[{'sha': 'ef7dcc044b50f31476f734b4b72fa0e5a5a0d5ee', 'message': 'Fix OpportunisticBatching overhead for low-resource pods

- Add podLikelyFitsOnNode() optimization to avoid expensive RunFilterPlugins
- Use conservative 1% threshold for very low-resource pods
- Preserve existing filter logic for pods with no resources
- Reduce scheduling latency by 5-20ms for optimized cases
- Add comprehensive test coverage

Fixes: #137721
Related KEP: KEP-5598'}, {'sha': '7755b1b620a6e6c94d1781b4d8cff1490ebb2fd2', 'message': 'Fix OpportunisticBatching overhead - Corrected optimization approach

- Replace podLikelyFitsOnNode with isVeryLowResourcePod for simpler logic
- Skip expensive filter check entirely for very low-resource pods (≤10m CPU, ≤10MB memory)
- Preserve batch state to enable effective batching instead of invalidating it
- Use conservative thresholds to avoid breaking existing scheduling logic
- Add comprehensive test coverage for the new optimization

This fix eliminates 5-20ms synchronous filtering overhead for very low-resource pods
while maintaining scheduler correctness and existing batching behavior.

Fixes: #137721
Related KEP: KEP-5598'}, {'sha': 'cbb6f621d0ff69186c2c47e55d9ec7f45b3e31cd', 'message': 'Fix OpportunisticBatching overhead - Final working version

- Add lightweight resource check for very low-resource pods (≤10m CPU, ≤10MB memory)
- Use podCanFitOnNodeResources() instead of expensive RunFilterPlugins
- Preserve batch state invalidation logic for correctness
- Eliminate 5-20ms synchronous filtering overhead for optimized pods
- Maintain scheduler correctness and existing batching behavior

This provides the intended performance optimization while ensuring
the batch state is properly managed for scheduling correctness.

Fixes: #137721
Related KEP: KEP-5598'}]

[
  {
    "type": "Bug",
    "description": "在 `purgeInitContainers` 函数中，当 `activeSandboxIDs` 集合为空时，`activeSandboxIDs.Has(status.PodSandboxID)` 的检查将始终为 false，导致所有 init 容器都会被跳过清理。这可能导致在需要清理 init 容器时（例如，旧的 sandbox 已被杀死，但没有处于 READY 状态的新 sandbox），init 容器残留，从而影响后续的 pod 同步逻辑。",
    "evidence": "在 `pkg/kubelet/kuberuntime/kuberuntime_container.go` 文件的 `purgeInitContainers` 函数中，`activeSandboxIDs` 集合仅包含状态为 `runtimeapi.PodSandboxState_SANDBOX_READY` 的 sandbox。如果 pod 正在创建新的 sandbox (`CreateSandbox` 为 true)，但新的 sandbox 尚未达到 READY 状态，或者旧的 sandbox 已被杀死，那么 `activeSandboxIDs` 可能为空。此时，对于任何 init 容器，`activeSandboxIDs.Has(status.PodSandboxID)` 都会返回 false，导致 `continue` 语句执行，跳过清理。这与函数注释中提到的“清理被杀死 sandbox 中的 init 容器”的目标相悖。",
    "advice": "建议修改逻辑，明确区分需要清理的容器。可以考虑基于 sandbox 状态或生命周期来决策。例如，可以清理那些 sandbox ID 不在当前 `podStatus.SandboxStatuses` 列表中的所有 init 容器，或者清理那些 sandbox 状态不是 `SANDBOX_READY` 的 init 容器。需要确保在创建新 sandbox 时，旧 sandbox 中的 init 容器能被正确清理。",
    "confidence": 0.85,
    "severity": "Medium"
  },
  {

commits:
[{'sha': 'ef7dcc044b50f31476f734b4b72fa0e5a5a0d5ee', 'message': 'Fix OpportunisticBatching overhead for low-resource pods

- Add podLikelyFitsOnNode() optimization to avoid expensive RunFilterPlugins
- Use conservative 1% threshold for very low-resource pods
- Preserve existing filter logic for pods with no resources
- Reduce scheduling latency by 5-20ms for optimized cases
- Add comprehensive test coverage

Fixes: #137721
Related KEP: KEP-5598'}, {'sha': '7755b1b620a6e6c94d1781b4d8cff1490ebb2fd2', 'message': 'Fix OpportunisticBatching overhead - Corrected optimization approach

- Replace podLikelyFitsOnNode with isVeryLowResourcePod for simpler logic
- Skip expensive filter check entirely for very low-resource pods (≤10m CPU, ≤10MB memory)
- Preserve batch state to enable effective batching instead of invalidating it
- Use conservative thresholds to avoid breaking existing scheduling logic
- Add comprehensive test coverage for the new optimization

This fix eliminates 5-20ms synchronous filtering overhead for very low-resource pods
while maintaining scheduler correctness and existing batching behavior.

Fixes: #137721
Related KEP: KEP-5598'}, {'sha': 'cbb6f621d0ff69186c2c47e55d9ec7f45b3e31cd', 'message': 'Fix OpportunisticBatching overhead - Final working version

- Add lightweight resource check for very low-resource pods (≤10m CPU, ≤10MB memory)
- Use podCanFitOnNodeResources() instead of expensive RunFilterPlugins
- Preserve batch state invalidation logic for correctness
- Eliminate 5-20ms synchronous filtering overhead for optimized pods
- Maintain scheduler correctness and existing batching behavior

This provides the intended performance optimization while ensuring
the batch state is properly managed for scheduling correctness.

Fixes: #137721
Related KEP: KEP-5598'}]

```json
[
  {
    "type": "Bug",
    "description": "在 `pkg/api/testing/validation.go` 的 `verifyValidationEquivalence` 函数中，新增的过滤逻辑错误地假设了 `field.Error` 类型存在 `ShortCircuitedInDV` 字段。根据项目背景和代码变更，`MarkShortCircuit()` 方法返回的是 `field.ErrorList`，而不是修改 `field.Error` 的某个字段。`err.ShortCircuitedInDV` 的访问会导致编译错误或运行时 panic，因为 `field.Error` 结构体没有这个字段。这会导致测试函数无法正确编译或运行，影响验证逻辑的等价性测试。",
    "evidence": "文件: pkg/api/testing/validation.go
@@ -421,12 +421,21 @@ func verifyValidationEquivalence(t *testing.T, expectedErrs field.ErrorList, run
 		testCtx := rest.WithAllDeclarativeEnforcedForTest(ctx)
 		allDeclarativeErrs := runValidations(testCtx)
 
+		// In this mode, strategy.go validation remove all hand written validations errors which are marked covered by DV.
+		// so we have to filter out errors which are filtered out by strategy.go.
+		// This is because DV will not return those errors due to short circuiting of validations at the parent node.
+		filteredExpectedErrors := make(field.ErrorList, 0, len(expectedErrs))
+		for _, err := range expectedErrs {
+			if !err.ShortCircuitedInDV {
+				filteredExpectedErrors = append(filteredExpectedErrors, err)
+			}
+		}",
    "advice": "需要检查 `field.Error` 类型的实际定义，以确定如何正确识别被声明式验证（DV）短路的错误。可能的方法是通过错误类型、标签或其他字段来识别。建议查看 `field.Error` 结构体的定义以及 `MarkShortCircuit()` 方法的实现，以了解它如何标记错误。然后，相应地调整过滤逻辑。例如，如果 `MarkShortCircuit()` 设置了某个特定的错误类型或字段，则应根据该条件进行过滤。",
    "confidence": 0.95,
    "severity": "High"
  },
  {
    "type": "Design",
    "description": "在 `pkg/apis/rbac/validation/validation.go` 中，`ValidateRoleBindingUpdate` 函数的变更将原有的硬编码不可变字段检查替换为使用 `validation.ValidateImmutableField` 并标记为 Alpha 和 CoveredByDeclarative。然而，在 `pkg/apis/rbac/v1/zz_generated.validations.go`（以及 v1alpha1、v1beta1）的相同函数中，新增了使用 `validate.Immutable(...).MarkAlpha().MarkShortCircuit()` 的字段附加验证。这可能导致重复的验证逻辑或冲突，因为现在有两个地方（手写验证和生成验证）都在检查 `roleRef` 的不可变性。根据项目背景，声明式验证（DV）旨在逐步取代手写验证，但此变更似乎同时修改了两者，可能导致行为不一致或错误重复。",
    "evidence": "文件: pkg/apis/rbac/validation/validation.go
@@ -162,9 +161,7 @@ func ValidateRoleBindingUpdate(roleBinding *rbac.RoleBinding, oldRoleBinding *rb
 	allErrs := ValidateRoleBinding(roleBinding)
 	allErrs = append(allErrs, validation.ValidateObjectMetaUpdate(&roleBinding.ObjectMeta, &oldRoleBinding.ObjectMeta, field.NewPath("metadata"))...)
 
-	if oldRoleBinding.RoleRef != roleBinding.RoleRef {
-		allErrs = append(allErrs, field.Invalid(field.NewPath("roleRef"), roleBinding.RoleRef, "cannot change roleRef"))
-	}
+ allErrs = append(allErrs, validation.ValidateImmutableField(roleBinding.RoleRef, oldRoleBinding.RoleRef, field.NewPath("roleRef")).WithOrigin("immutable").MarkAlpha().MarkCoveredByDeclarative()...)
 以及文件: pkg/apis/rbac/v1/zz_generated.validations.go
 @@ -236,6 +236,15 @@ func Validate_RoleBinding(ctx context.Context, op operation.Operation, fldPath *
 			if oldValueCorrelated && op.Type == operation.Update && (obj == oldObj || (obj != nil && oldObj != nil && *obj == *oldObj)) {
 				return nil
 			}
+			// call field-attached validations
+			earlyReturn := false
+			if e := validate.Immutable(ctx, op, fldPath, obj, oldObj).MarkAlpha().MarkShortCircuit(); len(e) != 0 {
+				errs = append(errs, e...)
+				earlyReturn = true
+			}
+			if earlyReturn {
+				return // do not proceed
+			}",
    "advice": "需要确保手写验证和生成验证之间的职责清晰。既然生成验证已经添加了 `validate.Immutable` 检查并标记了 `MarkShortCircuit`，那么手写验证中的相应检查可能应该被移除或调整，以避免重复。建议审查整个 RBAC 验证策略，确保对于 `roleRef` 字段，只有一处（很可能是生成验证）执行不可变性检查，并且手写验证要么移除该检查，要么确保其与生成验证协调（例如，通过标记为已覆盖）。这符合项目向声明式验证迁移的目标。",
    "confidence": 0.85,
    "severity": "Medium"
  }
]
```

```json
  {
    "type": "Bug",
    "description": "在 `isPreemptionAllowedForDomain` 方法中，当 `domain.Nodes()` 返回空切片时，访问 `domain.Nodes()[0]` 会导致索引越界恐慌。该方法假设域中至少有一个节点，但调用方可能传入一个空节点列表的域。",

In case if the algorithm will be extracted into share place\n \t// and used for Workload aware preemption, also need handle pod group as preemptor scenarios.\n-\treturn pl.isPreemptionAllowed(domain.Nodes()[0], victim, preemptor.Members()[0])\n+\treturn pl.isPreemptionAllowed(domain.Nodes()[0], victim, preemptor.Members()[0])\n }",
    "advice": "在访问 `domain.Nodes()[0]` 和 `preemptor.Members()[0]` 之前，应添加边界检查以确保切片不为空。如果切片为空，应返回 false 或适当的错误状态。",

    "evidence": "文件: pkg/scheduler/framework/plugins/defaultpreemption/default_preemption.go\n@@ -567,3 +567,3 @@\n \t// TODO: Handle scenario when preemptor is PodGroup. In case if the algorithm will be extracted into share place\n \t// and used for Workload aware preemption, also need handle pod group as preemptor scenarios.\n-\treturn pl.fh.RunFilterPluginsWithNominatedPods(ctx, podPreemptor.CycleState(), podPreemptor.GetPod(), nodes[0])\n+\treturn pl.fh.RunFilterPluginsWithNominatedPods(ctx, podPreemptor.CycleState(), podPreemptor.GetPod(), nodes[0])\n }",
    "advice": "在访问 `nodes[0]` 之前，应检查 `nodes` 切片的长度。如果为空，应返回一个表示调度失败的框架状态。",
    "confidence": 0.9,
    "severity": "High"
  },
  {
    "type": "Bug",
    "description": "在 `SelectVictimsOnDomain` 方法中，当 `domain.Nodes()` 返回空切片时，`nameToNode` 映射将为空。后续循环 `for _, nodeInfo := range domain.Nodes()` 将不会执行，导致 `nameToNode` 映射为空。然而，在 `removeVictim` 和 `addVictim` 函数中，代码尝试通过 `pi.GetPod().Spec.NodeName` 从 `nameToNode` 映射中获取 `nodeInfo`。如果 `nameToNode` 映射中不存在该节点名，`nodeInfo` 将为 nil，随后在 `nodeInfo.RemovePod` 或 `nodeInfo.AddPodInfo` 上的调用将导致空指针解引用恐慌。",
    "evidence": "文件: pkg/scheduler/framework/plugins/defaultpreemption/default_preemption.go\n@@ -236,3 +236,3 @@\n \tnameToNode := make(map[string]fwk.NodeInfo)\n \tfor _, nodeInfo := range domain.Nodes() {\n-\t\tnameToNode[nodeInfo.Node().Name] = nodeInfo\n+\t\tnameToNode[nodeInfo.Node().Name] = nodeInfo\n \t}",
    "advice": "应确保 `domain.Nodes()` 返回至少一个节点，或者在 `removeVictim` 和 `addVictim` 中从 `nameToNode` 映射获取节点信息时进行空值检查。如果节点信息不存在，应返回错误。",
    "confidence": 0.8,
    "severity": "High"
  },
```

    "advice": "建议使用能保证精度的格式说明符，例如对于 `float64` 使用 `%g` 或 `%f` 并指定足够精度（如 `%.17g`），对于 `float32` 使用 `%.9g`。或者，考虑使用 `strconv.FormatFloat` 函数，它可以更精确地控制格式。",

    "evidence": "文件: staging/src/k8s.io/code-generator/cmd/validation-gen/validators/limits.go\n// 在 minimumTagValidator.GetValidations 中：\nswitch t.Name.Name {\ncase \"uint32\":\n\tif uintVal > math.MaxUint32 {\n\t\treturn result, fmt.Errorf(\"value %d does not fit in uint32\", uintVal)\n\t}\ncase \"uint64\":\n\t// valid\n}\n// 以及 maximumTagValidator.GetValidations 中的类似代码。缺少对 uint8, uint16, int8, int16 的检查。",

  {
    "type": "Bug",
    "description": "在 `validation.go` 的 `toGolangSourceDataLiteral` 函数中，对 `float32` 和 `float64` 类型的处理使用了 `%v` 格式说明符。这可能导致生成的代码字面量不精确或格式不一致，例如科学计数法表示，影响生成代码的可读性和潜在的可移植性。在代码生成中，数值字面量的精确表示至关重要。",
    "evidence": "case float32:\n\t\tsw.Do(fmt.Sprintf(\"float32(%v)\", v), nil)\n\ncase float64:\n\t\tsw.Do(fmt.Sprintf(\"float64(%v)\", v), nil)",
    "advice": "建议使用 `%g` 或 `%f` 等更精确控制浮点数输出的格式说明符，例如 `fmt.Sprintf(\"float64(%g)\", v)`，以确保生成的代码字面量是精确且一致的。",

  },

    "description": "在 `limits.go` 中，新增的 `minimumTagValidator.GetValidations` 和 `maximumTagValidator.GetValidations` 方法逻辑结构重复。两个方法都包含了几乎相同的用于处理有符号整数和无符号整数的分支逻辑，包括解析、范围检查和添加验证函数。这种重复违反了 DRY（Don't Repeat Yourself）原则，增加了代码维护的复杂性和出错风险。",
    "evidence": "两个方法中都出现了以下模式：\nif isUnsignedInt(t) {\n    // 解析 uint64，进行 uint32 范围检查，添加函数\n} else {\n    // 解析 int64，进行 int32 范围检查，添加函数\n}",
    "advice": "建议将解析、范围检查和添加验证函数的公共逻辑提取为辅助函数（例如 `parseAndValidateIntegerBound`），供 `minimumTagValidator` 和 `maximumTagValidator` 调用，以减少代码重复并提高可维护性。",
    "confidence": 0.9,
    "severity": "Medium"
  },
  {
    "type": "Bug",
    "description": "在 `limits.go` 中，对 `uint32` 的范围检查不完整。代码只检查了 `uintVal > math.MaxUint32`，但没有检查 `uintVal` 是否为负数。虽然 `strconv.ParseUint` 对于负数字符串会返回错误，但为了逻辑的健壮性和清晰性，显式检查可能更安全。然而，更关键的问题是，对于有符号整数类型（如 `int`、`int16`、`int8`），代码只处理了 `int32` 和 `int64` 的范围检查，遗漏了 `int`、`int16`、`int8` 的检查。这可能导致为这些类型生成超出其范围的验证字面量，从而在运行时产生未定义行为或编译错误。",
    "evidence": "switch t.Name.Name {\ncase \"uint32\":\n\tif uintVal > math.MaxUint32 {\n\t\treturn result, fmt.Errorf(\"value %d does not fit in uint32\", uintVal)\n\t}\ncase \"uint64\":\n\t// valid\n}\n...\nswitch t.Name.Name {\ncase \"int32\":\n\tif intVal < math.MinInt32 || intVal > math.MaxInt32 {\n\t\treturn result, fmt.Errorf(\"value %d does not fit in int32\", intVal)\n\t}\ncase \"int64\":\n\t// valid\n}",
    "advice": "建议扩展范围检查逻辑，覆盖所有可能的有符号整数类型（`int`, `int8`, `int16`, `int32`, `int64`）和无符号整数类型（`uint`, `uint8`, `uint16`, `uint32`, `uint64`），确保为每个类型生成的值都在其有效范围内。可以使用 `math` 包中的常量（如 `math.MaxInt8`, `math.MinInt8`）来进行检查。",
    "confidence": 0.9,
    "severity": "High"
  },
  {
    "type": "Design",
    "description": "代码变更引入了一个设计不一致性问题。在 `validation.go` 中，`toGolangSourceDataLiteral` 函数现在为所有整数和浮点类型显式添加了类型转换（如 `int64(%d)`），这与之前直接输出值的做法不同。然而，对于 `bool` 类型，仍然使用 `%v` 直接输出。虽然 `bool` 类型通常不需要转换，但这种处理方式在风格上与其他类型不统一。更重要的是，在 `limits.go` 中，解析逻辑现在直接使用 `strconv.ParseUint` 和 `strconv.ParseInt`，而不再使用项目中原有的 `util.ParseInt` 辅助函数。这可能导致项目内部辅助函数使用不一致，如果 `util.ParseInt` 有额外的逻辑（如错误处理或格式支持），则可能被绕过。",
    "evidence": "在 `validation.go` 中：`case bool:\n\t\tsw.Do(fmt.Sprintf(\"%v\", v), nil)`。在 `limits.go` 中，移除了对 `util.ParseInt` 的调用，改为直接使用 `strconv.ParseUint` 和 `strconv.ParseInt`。",
    "advice": "1. 考虑是否也应为 `bool` 类型使用更明确的格式，例如 `fmt.Sprintf(\"%t\", v)`，以保持风格一致。2. 审查 `util.ParseInt` 函数，了解其是否包含必要的逻辑（例如处理不同进制或额外错误检查）。如果新逻辑（分有符号/无符号解析）是必要的且 `util.ParseInt` 不满足需求，那么这种变更是合理的，但应在提交信息或代码注释中说明原因。如果 `util.ParseInt` 仍有价值，应考虑重构它或在新逻辑中复用其部分功能。",
    "confidence": 0.7,
    "severity": "Low"
  }
]

review:
[
  {
    "type": "Bug",
    "description": "测试场景中显式设置了 NodeResourcesFit 插件的计分策略为 MostAllocated，这与调度器的默认策略（LeastAllocated）不同。虽然提交信息表明这是为了修复测试不稳定性，但代码变更本身存在一个潜在问题：在创建调度器配置时，使用了 `configtesting.V1ToInternalWithDefaults(t, ...)` 函数。此函数会应用默认值，但默认值可能包含其他插件配置。如果默认配置中已经存在 NodeResourcesFit 插件的配置项，此处的显式设置可能会与之冲突或导致配置合并行为不符合预期，从而可能影响测试的稳定性和可预测性。",
    "evidence": "文件 `tas_test.go` 第 1303-1322 行：`schedCfg := configtesting.V1ToInternalWithDefaults(t, configv1.KubeSchedulerConfiguration{...})`。这里创建了一个包含显式 NodeResourcesFit 插件配置的调度器配置。",
    "advice": "建议审查 `configtesting.V1ToInternalWithDefaults` 函数的行为，确认它如何处理传入配置与默认配置的合并。更安全的做法可能是直接构建完整的内部配置对象，或者使用一个专门用于测试的配置构建函数，该函数能确保只应用测试所需的配置，避免与全局默认值产生不可预见的交互。",
    "confidence": 0.7,
    "severity": "Medium"
  },
  {
    "type": "Readability",
    "description": "新增的代码块（用于配置调度器计分策略）直接嵌入在 `runTestScenario` 函数中。这段配置代码较长且具体，使得主测试函数的逻辑变得臃肿，降低了可读性。考虑到 Kubernetes 项目中其他测试文件可能也有类似的配置需求，这段代码存在重复的可能性。",
    "evidence": "文件 `tas_test.go` 第 1303-1322 行：新增了一个用于创建 `schedCfg` 的代码块，包含了对 `configv1.KubeSchedulerConfiguration` 的详细构造。",
    "advice": "建议将调度器配置的创建逻辑（特别是针对 MostAllocated 策略的配置）提取到一个独立的辅助函数中，例如 `makeSchedulerConfigWithMostAllocated()`。这样可以提高代码的可读性、可维护性，并便于在其他测试中复用。",
    "confidence": 0.8,
    "severity": "Low"
  }
]

review:
[
  {
    "type": "Bug",
    "description": "在 `cache.go` 文件中，新增的代理函数覆盖了原有的 `config.Proxy` 逻辑。原代码会根据 `config.Proxy` 是否为 nil 来决定使用自定义代理函数还是环境变量代理。新代码直接定义了一个新的代理函数，完全忽略了 `config.Proxy` 字段。这会导致任何通过 `Config.Proxy` 设置的代理配置失效，破坏了原有功能。",
    "evidence": "文件: staging/src/k8s.io/client-go/transport/cache.go
@@ -139,9 +141,19 @@ func (c *tlsTransportCache) get(config *Config) (http.RoundTripper, error) {
 		go dynamicCertDialer.run(ctx.Done())
 	}
 
-	proxy := http.ProxyFromEnvironment
-	if config.Proxy != nil {
-		proxy = config.Proxy
+	proxy := func(req *http.Request) (*url.URL, error) {
+		u, err := http.ProxyFromEnvironment(req)
+		klog.Infof("DEBUG proxy decision: url=%s host=%s NO_PROXY=%q no_proxy=%q HTTPS_PROXY=%q https_proxy=%q proxy=%v err=%v",
+			req.URL.String(),
+			req.URL.Host,
+			os.Getenv("NO_PROXY"),
+			os.Getenv("no_proxy"),
+			os.Getenv("HTTPS_PROXY"),
+			os.Getenv("https_proxy"),
+			u,
+			err,
+		)

commits:
[{'sha': 'b22d00b401c88e726c15517c08e00c3299753b6b', 'message': 'Fix goroutine leaks in ephemeral volume controller test

Use context.WithCancel and properly shut down the informer factory
and workqueue in TestSyncHandler to prevent goroutine leaks.

Previously, the test used context.Background() which never cancels,
leaving informer and workqueue goroutines running after test completion.
Now that context support has been added to tools/cache (#126387),
the informers can be cleanly shut down via context cancellation.

Also add goleak.VerifyTestMain to detect goroutine leak regressions.'}]

', 'MaxLenError, RegexError, and EmptyError were duplicated between pkg/util/validation and pkg/api/validate/content. The content package is the canonical location for these helpers. The local copies in validation.go were independent implementations that could diverge silently — and already had: MaxLenError in validation.go returned "characters" while content.MaxLenError returns "bytes", which is incorrect for multi-byte UTF-8 strings. This PR replaces the local implementations with thin wrappers that delegate to content, and marks them as deprecated so callers can migrate to the canonical package directly.', '/kind cleanup ', '```release-note Validation error messages from MaxLenError now say "bytes" instead of "characters" for consistency with pkg/api/validate/content. ```']

commits:
[{'sha': '24c232d6b1aeab01407c073334a99bb3fa463902', 'message': 'localupcluster: properly query /readyz and /healthz

Despite being called checkReadiness, the function was only performing
a liveness check: /healthz was polled over HTTPS without verifying the
certificate or authenticating, and any HTTP response was accepted as a
signal that the component was up. The only exception was kubelet,
where a node readiness check was added on top.

Switched to /readyz for kube-apiserver and kube-scheduler,
kept /healthz for the rest and require HTTP 200 in all cases.

This ensures that the kube-apiserver is fully initialized before
dependent components are started.'}, {'sha': '51d0c8843d6789ccbc056a2f5d41c5cfa23147db', 'message': "localupcluster: set readiness polling interval to 1 second

Without an explicit interval, Gomega's default polling is very frequent,
generating a large volume of /readyz and /healthz requests in the component
logs. Set an explicit 1-second interval to reduce noise while still
detecting readiness promptly."}, {'sha': 'e3aa2b9b2930403456d50d80421b6e241788e695', 'message': 'test/localupcluster: stop all components before starting replacements

Modify() was replacing components one at a time: stop X, start X, stop Y,
start Y, ... in version-skew order (apiserver last on downgrade). This
caused a crash during downgrade: KCM-1.35 started against the still-
running apiserver-1.36, passed its /healthz, and then immediately lose
its connection when apiserver-1.36 was killed by the localupcluster.
KCM-1.35 would reconnect to the not-yet-ready apiserver-1.35, hit a
403 RBAC error during controller initialization, and exit — because that
initialization phase does not retry on RBAC errors.

Fix by splitting Modify() into two phases:

  Phase 1 — stop all components to be replaced, in reverse startup order
  (kube-proxy down to apiserver), so dependent components release their
  connections before the apiserver is stopped.

  Phase 2 — start all replacement components in standard startup order
  (apiserver first), so each component connects to a fully-ready apiserver.'}]

  {
    "type": "Bug",
    "description": "在 `Modify` 函数中，`restore` 变量的 `FileByComponent` 字段被初始化为空映射，但后续从未被填充。这意味着当需要回滚时，`restore` 选项无法知道哪些组件被修改过，也无法恢复其原始二进制文件路径。这可能导致 `Modify` 函数无法正确恢复集群状态，违反了该函数的设计意图（即返回一个可用于恢复的 `ModifyOptions`）。",
    "evidence": "文件: test/utils/localupcluster/localupcluster.go
@@ -373,24 +369,19 @@ func (c *Cluster) Modify(tCtx ktesting.TContext, state string, options ModifyOpt
 		FileByComponent: make(map[KubeComponentName]string),
 	}
 
-	restore.Upgrade = !options.Upgrade
-	components := slices.Clone(KubeClusterComponents)
-	if !options.Upgrade {
-		slices.Reverse(components)
-	}
-	for _, component := range components {
-		c.modifyComponent(tCtx, state, options, component, &restore)
-	}
-	return restore
-}
-
-func (c *Cluster) modifyComponent(tCtx ktesting.TContext, state string, options ModifyOptions, component KubeComponentName, restore *ModifyOptions) {
-	tCtx.Helper()
-	tCtx = tCtx.WithStep(fmt.Sprintf("modify %s", component))
-
 	// We could also do things like turning feature gates on or off.

-	if fileName := options.GetComponentFile(component); fileName != "" {
+	updated := make(map[KubeComponentName]*Cmd)
+
+	// Phase 1: stop all components that need modification in reverse order
+	// so that dependent components (KCM, scheduler) are stopped before the
+	// apiserver they depend on.
+	for _, component := range slices.Backward(KubeClusterComponents) {
+		fileName := options.GetComponentFile(component)
+		if fileName == "" {
+			continue
+		}
+		tCtx := tCtx.WithStep(fmt.Sprintf("stop %s", component))

 		cmd.Stop(tCtx)
 		delete(c.running, component)
 
-		restore.FileByComponent[component] = cmd.File
+		restore.FileByComponent[component] = cmd.File
 
 		cmdLine := slices.Clone(cmd.CommandLine)
 		cmdLine[0] = fileName
 		cmd.File = fileName
 		cmd.Name = string(component) + "-" + state
 		cmd.CommandLine = cmdLine
 		cmd.LogFile = path.Join(c.dir, fmt.Sprintf("%s-%s.log", component, state))
+		updated[component] = cmd
 	}
 
-		c.runComponentWithRetry(tCtx, component, cmd)
+	// Phase 2: start all stopped components in the standard startup order
+	// (apiserver first) so that each component starts against a fully-ready
+	// apiserver.
+	for _, component := range KubeClusterComponents {
+		if cmd, ok := updated[component]; ok {
+			c.runComponentWithRetry(tCtx, component, cmd)
+		}
 	}
+
+	return restore
 }",
    "advice": "在 `Phase 1` 的循环中，当 `fileName` 不为空时，应该将原始组件的文件路径（`cmd.File`）记录到 `restore.FileByComponent[component]` 中。这确保了 `restore` 选项包含恢复所需的信息。请添加这行代码：`restore.FileByComponent[component] = cmd.File`。",

commits:
[{'sha': '24c232d6b1aeab01407c073334a99bb3fa463902', 'message': 'localupcluster: properly query /readyz and /healthz

Despite being called checkReadiness, the function was only performing
a liveness check: /healthz was polled over HTTPS without verifying the
certificate or authenticating, and any HTTP response was accepted as a
signal that the component was up. The only exception was kubelet,
where a node readiness check was added on top.

Switched to /readyz for kube-apiserver and kube-scheduler,
kept /healthz for the rest and require HTTP 200 in all cases.

This ensures that the kube-apiserver is fully initialized before
dependent components are started.'}, {'sha': '51d0c8843d6789ccbc056a2f5d41c5cfa23147db', 'message': "localupcluster: set readiness polling interval to 1 second

Without an explicit interval, Gomega's default polling is very frequent,
generating a large volume of /readyz and /healthz requests in the component
logs. Set an explicit 1-second interval to reduce noise while still
detecting readiness promptly."}, {'sha': 'e3aa2b9b2930403456d50d80421b6e241788e695', 'message': 'test/localupcluster: stop all components before starting replacements

Modify() was replacing components one at a time: stop X, start X, stop Y,
start Y, ... in version-skew order (apiserver last on downgrade). This
caused a crash during downgrade: KCM-1.35 started against the still-
running apiserver-1.36, passed its /healthz, and then immediately lose
its connection when apiserver-1.36 was killed by the localupcluster.
KCM-1.35 would reconnect to the not-yet-ready apiserver-1.35, hit a
403 RBAC error during controller initialization, and exit — because that
initialization phase does not retry on RBAC errors.

Fix by splitting Modify() into two phases:

  Phase 1 — stop all components to be replaced, in reverse startup order
  (kube-proxy down to apiserver), so dependent components release their
  connections before the apiserver is stopped.

  Phase 2 — start all replacement components in standard startup order
  (apiserver first), so each component connects to a fully-ready apiserver.'}]

['~does this mean restarting the kubelet at inopportune times can corrupt the user namespace state file in non-test scenarios?~ ~if so... should that be fixed in a more fundamental way at the point where the user namespace state file is getting corrupted, rather than resolving by making particular tests coordinate restart more carefully?~ edit: I reread the description and saw the issue was reconfiguring the kubelet without cleaning up state', 'I re-read the description ... it looks like the "corruption" comes from reconfiguring the kubelet without cleaning up the generated on-disk state files related to that configuration. The updateKubeletConfig method takes a `deleteStateFiles bool` param that is *supposed* to remove state files when changing kubelet config, right? Would it be more robust to include cleaning up the "pod's directory on disk (specifically the userns mapping file)" when `deleteStateFiles=true`?', 'it seems like the corruption could happen in both directions, right? * any uncleaned pod state from a test *prior* to the custom mapping test would trigger invalid config *with* the custom mapping * any uncleaned pod state *with* the custom mapping would trigger invalid config with the *default* mapping in tests that happen *afterwards*', "> it seems like the corruption could happen in both directions, right? Hypothetically, yes, but this is a serial test, so everything except system pods should be cleaned up first, and I don't think any of the system pods run with `HostUsers: false`.", '> Would it be more robust to include cleaning up the "pod's directory on disk (specifically the userns mapping file)" when deleteStateFiles=true? Yeah, that makes sense. Unfortunately the user namespace manager uses a different structure for state than than the other stateful managers, with files embedded in the pod directory, so cleanup is less straightforward. I'll make the change though.', "Actually, I'd prefer to proceed with the current fix (`DeleteSync`), since it keeps the change localized to the problematic test, and is much smaller change. I think deleting the userns state files might be correct, but I'd rather address that as a follow-up.", "+1, let's keep this scoped for code freeze, maybe file a bug to track the follow-up?", '/lgtm', '/assign @liggitt /cc @BenTheElder ', '/retest', '/assign @haircommander ', '/hold for the question about where the "corrupted" user namespace state file issue should be fixed', 'LGTM label has been added.

', '/approve /triage accepted', "OK, I missed that the test is already doing a DeleteSync. After more tracing, here's what is actually going on: If a graceful deletion is issued before the pod has terminated, it delegates the actual deletion to the Kublet. That happens in `SyncTerminatedPod` right **after** the user namespace file is cleaned up: https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/blob/61d4e7ae861df14559c338ec37071ac495bde6db/pkg/kubelet/kubelet.go#L2512-L2515 Normally there is a few seconds lag after the pod terminates before it's status is updated, so DeleteSync correctly blocks test cleanup until the pod been deleted by the Kubelet, after the userns file is cleaned up: https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/blob/61d4e7ae861df14559c338ec37071ac495bde6db/test/e2e/framework/pod/output/output.go#L176-L183 However, with `PLEGOnDemandRelist`, the pod is marked as `Succeeded` immediately, and the delete request immediately removes the pod: https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/blob/61d4e7ae861df14559c338ec37071ac495bde6db/pkg/registry/core/pod/strategy.go#L162-L178 Which unblocks the test completion, letting the Kubelet be shut down before SyncTerminatedPod is called. I verified the order of events from the Kubelet log: `SyncLoop REMOVE` is called (on pod removed from API) before `SyncTerminatedPod`. I think this is all working as intended, so I'm going to proceed with @liggitt's original suggestion to just clean up the state files as part of the Kubelet's config update.", 'Pushed the updated state file clean up. Would it be more robust to include cleaning up the "pod's directory on disk (specifically the userns mapping file)" when `deleteStateFiles=true`?', 'it seems like the corruption could happen in both directions, right? * any uncleaned pod state from a test *prior* to the custom mapping test would trigger invalid config *with* the custom mapping * any uncleaned pod state *with* the custom mapping would trigger invalid config with the *default* mapping in tests that happen *afterwards*', "> it seems like the corruption could happen in both directions, right? Hypothetically, yes, but this is a serial test, so everything except system pods should be cleaned up first, and I don't think any of the system pods run with `HostUsers: false`.", '> Would it be more robust to include cleaning up the "pod's directory on disk (specifically the userns mapping file)" when deleteStateFiles=true? Yeah, that makes sense. Unfortunately the user namespace manager uses a different structure for state than than the other stateful managers, with files embedded in the pod directory, so cleanup is less straightforward. I'll make the change though.', "Actually, I'd prefer to proceed with the current fix (`DeleteSync`), since it keeps the change localized to the problematic test, and is much smaller change. ', '/approve /triage accepted', "OK, I missed that the test is already doing a DeleteSync. After more tracing, here's what is actually going on: If a graceful deletion is issued before the pod has terminated, it delegates the actual deletion to the Kublet. That happens in `SyncTerminatedPod` right **after** the user namespace file is cleaned up: https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/blob/61d4e7ae861df14559c338ec37071ac495bde6db/pkg/kubelet/kubelet.go#L2512-L2515 Normally there is a few seconds lag after the pod terminates before it's status is updated, so DeleteSync correctly blocks test cleanup until the pod been deleted by the Kubelet, after the userns file is cleaned up: https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/blob/61d4e7ae861df14559c338ec37071ac495bde6db/test/e2e/framework/pod/output/output.go#L176-L183 However, with `PLEGOnDemandRelist`, the pod is marked as `Succeeded` immediately, and the delete request immediately removes the pod: https://github.com/kubernetes/kubernetes/blob/61d4e7ae861df14559c338ec37071ac495bde6db/pkg/registry/core/pod/strategy.go#L162-L178 Which unblocks the test completion, letting the Kubelet be shut down before SyncTerminatedPod is called. I verified the order of events from the Kubelet log: `SyncLoop REMOVE` is called (on pod removed from API) before `SyncTerminatedPod`. I think this is all working as intended, so I'm going to proceed with @liggitt's original suggestion to just clean up the state files as part of the Kubelet's config update.", 'Pushed the updated state file clean up. commits:
[{'sha': 'b93ba52b9cd76063ff18fa65ccdf5bfb45526fa9', 'message': 'cri-client: use passthrough resolver for socket endpoints

gRPC defaults to the DNS resolver for bare targets passed to
NewClient. For CRI socket endpoints, GetAddressAndDialer returns a
socket path plus a custom dialer, but handing the bare path to
grpc.NewClient still lets gRPC resolve the target first.

That breaks unix socket clients with errors like "name resolver error:
produced zero addresses" before the custom dialer ever sees the raw
path. Use the passthrough resolver for socket-style addresses so the
runtime and image clients hand the original endpoint directly to the
custom dialer.

Add a regression test for unix sockets, Windows named pipes, and TCP
addresses.

Precedent:
https://github.com/etcd-io/etcd/blob/v3.3.27/clientv3/client.go#L266-L270
https://github.com/grpc/grpc-go/blob/v1.72.2/dialoptions.go#L448-L451

Signed-off-by: Davanum Srinivas <davanum@gmail.com>'}, {'sha': '8298942a3ab1c5dbdd35998375da912057f407f9', 'message': 'cri-client: add unix socket constructor tests

Signed-off-by: Davanum Srinivas <davanum@gmail.com>'}, {'sha': '022a649b110c45f1685a841ed70335bee1b7c6da', 'message': 'cri-client: normalize unix socket endpoints in target helper'}]
